title: My first morning in a rented room abroad
summary: Describe the small details of waking up for the first time in an unfamiliar rented room in another country.
topic: My first morning in a rented room abroad
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences describing the first morning you woke up in a rented room in another city or country. Focus on small, specific details — what you saw, heard, or smelled. Use present simple or past simple.
hintStarters: When I woke up, I did not know where I was for a moment because…
The room was very small, with only…
Through the window, I could see…
The sound I heard first was…

hintVocab.meaningVi: an cư, ổn định, thích nghi
đồ đạc, tài sản cá nhân

sampleText: The first morning I woke up in my rented room in Lisbon, I stared at the ceiling for a long time and did not know where I was. The room had thin white walls and a window with no curtain, so light came in early and woke me before seven. Outside, I could hear a tram moving slowly along the street below, and somewhere in the building, pipes made a loud knocking sound. The room smelled a little damp, the way old buildings often do in autumn. My two suitcases were in the corner, still closed. I had not unpacked anything yet. On the small table there was a key, a printed sheet with the Wi-Fi password, and nothing else. The bed was narrow but comfortable enough. I got up and looked out of the window. The street was quiet. A man in a blue jacket was opening the shutters of a café across the road. He moved slowly and looked like he did this every morning. I watched him for a few minutes, then I went to find the shower. The water was warm. Somehow, that small thing was enough to make the room feel a little more like mine.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nhìn chằm chằm lên trần nhà

sampleAnnotations.phrase: did not know where I was
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không biết mình đang ở đâu
một cửa sổ không có rèm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: pipes made a loud knocking sound
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ống nước tạo ra tiếng gõ to

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the way old buildings often do
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: theo cái cách các tòa nhà cũ thường có

sampleAnnotations.phrase: moved slowly and looked like he did this every morning
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: di chuyển chậm rãi và trông như thể ông làm điều này mỗi sáng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: make the room feel a little more like mine
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Điều đó đủ để khiến căn phòng cảm thấy
giống của mình hơn một chút

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple and past perfect for sequence
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Past simple ('woke up', 'came in') dùng để kể hành động xảy ra lần lượt. Past perfect ('had not unpacked') dùng để chỉ điều gì đó chưa xảy ra trước thời điểm đó trong câu chuyện. Ví dụ: 'I had not unpacked anything yet' nghĩa là việc mở vali chưa xảy ra vào buổi sáng đó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Past simple ('woke up', 'came in') tells events in order. Past perfect ('had not unpacked') shows something had not happened yet before that story moment. Example: 'I had not unpacked anything yet' means the unpacking had not taken place on that morning.

sampleTranslationVi: Buổi sáng đầu tiên thức dậy trong phòng thuê ở Lisbon, tôi nhìn chằm chằm lên trần nhà một lúc lâu và không biết mình đang ở đâu. Căn phòng có những bức tường trắng mỏng và một cửa sổ không có rèm, vì vậy ánh sáng vào sớm và đánh thức tôi trước bảy giờ. Bên ngoài, tôi nghe thấy tàu điện di chuyển chậm dọc theo con phố bên dưới, và đâu đó trong tòa nhà, ống nước tạo ra tiếng gõ to. Căn phòng có mùi hơi ẩm, theo cái cách các tòa nhà cũ thường có vào mùa thu. Hai chiếc vali của tôi ở góc phòng, vẫn còn đóng. Tôi vẫn chưa mở vali ra. Trên chiếc bàn nhỏ có một chiếc chìa khóa, một tờ giấy in có mật khẩu Wi-Fi, và không có gì khác. Chiếc giường hẹp nhưng đủ thoải mái. Tôi dậy và nhìn ra cửa sổ. Con phố yên tĩnh. Một người đàn ông mặc áo khoác xanh đang mở cửa chớp của một quán cà phê bên kia đường. Ông di chuyển chậm rãi và trông như thể ông làm điều này mỗi sáng. Tôi nhìn ông vài phút, rồi đi tìm phòng tắm. Nước ấm. Điều đó đủ để khiến căn phòng cảm thấy giống của mình hơn một chút.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about waking up and feeling disoriented.
options: I woke up and did not know where I am.
I woke up and did not know where I was.
I woke up and don't know where I was.
I woke up and was not knowing where I was.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs should be past simple: 'woke up' and 'did not know'. 'Did not know where I was' is correct; 'where I am' mixes past and present.
mcQuestions.hint: In a past story, what tense should the second verb be?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes light entering a room?
options: Light came in early and woke me up.
Light was come in early and woke me up.
Light came in early and has woken me up.
Light come in early and woke me up.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Came in' is the correct past simple of 'come in'. Both verbs in the sequence use past simple.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'come'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence using past perfect to show something had not been done yet.
options: I not unpacked anything yet.
I didn't unpack anything yet.
I hadn't unpacked anything yet.
I have not unpacked anything yet.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'hadn't unpacked' is correct for something that had not happened before a past moment. 'Have not' is present perfect and breaks the past narrative.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense shows something had not happened before an earlier past moment?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the correct phrase for the sound of someone's feet on the floor.
options: I heard footsteps in the corridor.
I heard footstep in the corridor.
I heard a footsteps in the corridor.
I heard footsteps on the corridor.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Footsteps' is plural and uncountable in this context (no article needed). The correct preposition is 'in the corridor', not 'on'.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'footsteps' countable here? What preposition goes with 'corridor'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'look like' to describe a person's appearance.
options: He looked like he did this every morning.
He looked like he does this every morning.
He looked as he did this every morning.
He was looking like he did this every morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Looked like + clause' with past tense in the clause is correct. 'As' is not used with 'looked like' in this pattern.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'look like' take 'as' or stay as 'look like' before a clause?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct way to say that something was enough to cause a change.
options: That was enough for make the room feel like mine.
That was enough making the room feel like mine.
That was enough to make the room feel like mine.
That was enough that make the room feel like mine.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Enough + to + infinitive' is the correct structure: 'enough to make'.
mcQuestions.hint: What follows 'enough' when you want to express a purpose or result?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word best describes a room that feels slightly wet or humid?
options: damp
thin
narrow
temporary
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Damp' means slightly wet or humid. 'Thin' describes thickness; 'narrow' describes width; 'temporary' means not permanent.
mcQuestions.hint: Which adjective describes moisture in the air of an old building?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence describing something happening through a window.
options: Through the window, I could see the street.
Through the window, I can see the street.
Through the window, I saw able the street.
Through the window, I was able see the street.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Could see' is the past form of 'can see', meaning ability in the past. 'Was able to see' is also correct but option A is more natural here.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past form of 'can'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about a person opening a shop in the morning.
options: He was opening the shutters of a café.
He opened the shutters of a café.
He opens the shutters of a café.
He has opened the shutters of a café.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was opening' is correct here because the writer watched the action in progress. Past simple 'opened' would also be acceptable but describes a completed event.
mcQuestions.hint: Was the opening in progress when the writer watched, or was it a completed action?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct way to say that belongings are still closed in the corner.
options: My suitcases were in the corner, yet closed.
My suitcases were in the corner, still closed.
My suitcases were in the corner, always closed.
My suitcases were in the corner, even closed.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Still closed' means they had not been opened yet — 'still' is the correct adverb for a state continuing up to that moment.
mcQuestions.hint: Which adverb shows that a state has not changed up to the present moment?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the correct description of a bed that is not very wide.
options: The bed was thin but comfortable.
The bed was narrow but comfortable.
The bed was slim but comfortable.
The bed was tight but comfortable.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Narrow' describes something with a small width, like a bed or a street. 'Thin' and 'slim' refer to people or flat objects; 'tight' means with little space.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word describes a bed that is not wide?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Think of a room you stayed in for the first time — in a new city, a hostel, or a relative's house. What detail do you remember most clearly, and why do you think that detail stayed with you?
